accurate.why: All claims match the bill text or are accurate context about the War Powers Resolution framework and statutes cited.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; statutory references and findings are correctly reflected from the bill.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the primary purpose: directing removal of U.S. forces from unauthorized hostilities against Iran.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly notes the resolution invokes existing War Powers procedures (1013 of the State Authorization Act and 601(b) of ISAAECA) rather than amending them.

exceptions_conditions.why: Captures the key carve-outs in the rule of construction (self-defense, intelligence, partner defense, evacuation) and the authorization exception.

effective_dates.why: Bill specifies no effective date or deadline for removal; relevant dates (Feb 28, Apr 8) are noted accurately in the findings discussion.

major_provisions_covered.why: Covers the operative directive, findings, statutory basis, and rule of construction without misrepresentation.

objective_tone.why: Neutral, non-partisan description of findings and provisions without editorializing.

coherent.why: Well-organized and internally consistent across the operative directive, findings, and exceptions.

concise.why: Compact with no padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: Correctly names the statutes, the War Powers Resolution, Strait of Hormuz, and Operation Epic Fury as stated in the bill.

correct_figures.why: Dates cited (February 28, 2026 and April 8, 2026) match the bill's findings.
